methods.description: Experimental setup for studying the effects of soil contamination and remediation using legume plants in micro-field conditions.

steps.description: Film vessels with a hole in the bottom part were used for creating models.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare film vessels with drainage holes for soil experiments

steps.description: The soil was taken from the upper plowing horizon in the quantity of 5 kg/vessel.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and measure 5 kg of soil from upper plowing horizon for each vessel

steps.description: Highly soluble salts of zinc, copper, lead, and cadmium were separately dispensed in concentrations corresponding to the bottom boundary of 'very severe' level of soil contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: dispense metal salts into soil to achieve 'very severe' contamination levels

steps.description: The application of the diatomic ore material and the inoculation of seed with the bacterial preparation were done before seeding.
steps.searchable_summary: apply diatomic ore and inoculate seeds with bacterial preparation before planting

methods.description: Analysis of the first hay crop of legume grasses to assess the quality of products and the role of diatomite in soil remediation.

steps.description: In laboratory conditions, the first hay crop of legume grasses was analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze first hay crop of legume grasses in laboratory

steps.description: The quality of products was assessed according to VMDU-87 Regulation 'Temporary Maximum Allowable Limit (MAL) of the Content of Certain Chemical Elements and of Gossypol in Feedstuff for Agricultural Animals and Feedstuff Additives'.
steps.searchable_summary: assess product quality using VMDU-87 Regulation standards

steps.description: According to the content of metals in plants, the role of diatomite as the remediator of contaminated soils was assessed.
steps.searchable_summary: assess diatomite's role in soil remediation based on metal content in plants
